verses.esv: And immediately the king sent an executioner with orders to bring John's head. He went and beheaded him in the prison
verses.kjv: And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
verses.bsb: So without delay, the king commanded that John’s head be brought in. He sent an executioner, who went and beheaded him in the prison.

verses.esv: and brought his head on a platter and gave it to the girl, and the girl gave it to her mother.
verses.kjv: And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.
verses.bsb: The man brought John’s head on a platter and presented it to the girl, who gave it to her mother.

verses.esv: When his disciples heard of it, they came and took his body and laid it in a tomb.
verses.kjv: And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
verses.bsb: When John’s disciples heard about this, they came and took his body and placed it in a tomb.

verses.esv: The apostles returned to Jesus and told him all that they had done and taught.
verses.kjv: And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
verses.bsb: Meanwhile, the apostles gathered around Jesus and brought Him news of all they had done and taught.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Come away by yourselves to a desolate place and rest a while.” For many were coming and going, and they had no leisure even to eat.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “Come with Me privately to a solitary place, and let us rest for a while.” For many people were coming and going, and they did not even have time to eat.

verses.esv: And they went away in the boat to a desolate place by themselves.
verses.kjv: And they departed into a desert place by ship privately.
verses.bsb: So they went away in a boat by themselves to a solitary place.

verses.esv: Now many saw them going and recognized them, and they ran there on foot from all the towns and got there ahead of them.
verses.kjv: And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.
verses.bsb: But many people saw them leaving and recognized them. They ran together on foot from all the towns and arrived before them.

verses.esv: When he went ashore he saw a great crowd, and he had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. And he began to teach them many things.
verses.kjv: And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd: and he began to teach them many things.
verses.bsb: When Jesus stepped ashore and saw a large crowd, He had compassion on them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. And He began to teach them many things.

verses.esv: And when it grew late, his disciples came to him and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is now late.
verses.kjv: And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:
verses.bsb: By now the hour was already late. So the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is already late.

verses.esv: Send them away to go into the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”
verses.kjv: Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.
verses.bsb: Dismiss the crowd so they can go to the surrounding countryside and villages and buy themselves something to eat.”

verses.esv: But he answered them, “You give them something to eat.” And they said to him, “Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give it to them to eat?”
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
verses.bsb: But Jesus told them, “You give them something to eat.”  They asked Him, “Should we go out and spend two hundred denarii to give all of them bread to eat?”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “How many loaves do you have? Go and see.” And when they had found out, they said, “Five, and two fish.”
verses.kjv: He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
verses.bsb: “Go and see how many loaves you have,” He told them.  And after checking, they said, “Five—and two fish.”

verses.esv: Then he commanded them all to sit down in groups on the green grass.
verses.kjv: And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus directed them to have the people sit in groups on the green grass.

verses.esv: So they sat down in groups, by hundreds and by fifties.
verses.kjv: And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.
verses.bsb: So they sat down in groups of hundreds and fifties.

verses.esv: And taking the five loaves and the two fish, he looked up to heaven and said a blessing and broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples to set before the people. And he divided the two fish among them all.
verses.kjv: And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.
verses.bsb: Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, Jesus spoke a blessing and broke the loaves. Then He gave them to His disciples to set before the people. And He divided the two fish among them all.

verses.esv: And they all ate and were satisfied.
verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled.
verses.bsb: They all ate and were satisfied,

verses.esv: And they took up twelve baskets full of broken pieces and of the fish.
verses.kjv: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
verses.bsb: and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces of bread and fish.

verses.esv: And those who ate the loaves were five thousand men.
verses.kjv: And they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men.
verses.bsb: And there were five thousand men who had eaten the loaves.

verses.esv: Immediately he made his disciples get into the boat and go before him to the other side, to Bethsaida, while he dismissed the crowd.
verses.kjv: And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
verses.bsb: Immediately Jesus made His disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of Him to Bethsaida, while He dismissed the crowd.

verses.esv: And after he had taken leave of them, he went up on the mountain to pray.
verses.kjv: And when he had sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.
verses.bsb: After bidding them farewell, He went up on the mountain to pray.

verses.esv: And when evening came, the boat was out on the sea, and he was alone on the land.
verses.kjv: And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.
verses.bsb: When evening came, the boat was in the middle of the sea, and Jesus was alone on land.

verses.esv: And he saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them. And about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea. He meant to pass by them,
verses.kjv: And he saw them toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.
verses.bsb: He could see that the disciples were straining to row, because the wind was against them. About the fourth watch of the night, Jesus went out to them, walking on the sea. He intended to pass by them,

verses.esv: but when they saw him walking on the sea they thought it was a ghost, and cried out,
verses.kjv: But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:
verses.bsb: but when they saw Him walking on the sea, they cried out, thinking He was a ghost—

verses.esv: for they all saw him and were terrified. But immediately he spoke to them and said, “Take heart; it is I. Do not be afraid.”
verses.kjv: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
verses.bsb: for they all saw Him and were terrified.  But Jesus spoke up at once: “Take courage! It is I. Do not be afraid.”

verses.esv: And he got into the boat with them, and the wind ceased. And they were utterly astounded,
verses.kjv: And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.
verses.bsb: Then He climbed into the boat with them, and the wind died down. And the disciples were utterly astounded,

verses.esv: for they did not understand about the loaves, but their hearts were hardened.
verses.kjv: For they considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was hardened.
verses.bsb: for they had not understood about the loaves, but their hearts had been hardened.

verses.esv: When they had crossed over, they came to land at Gennesaret and moored to the shore.
verses.kjv: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.
verses.bsb: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret and moored the boat.

verses.esv: And when they got out of the boat, the people immediately recognized him
verses.kjv: And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,
verses.bsb: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus

verses.esv: and ran about the whole region and began to bring the sick people on their beds to wherever they heard he was.
verses.kjv: And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.
verses.bsb: and ran through that whole region, carrying the sick on mats to wherever they heard He was.

verses.esv: And wherever he came, in villages, cities, or countryside, they laid the sick in the marketplaces and implored him that they might touch even the fringe of his garment. And as many as touched it were made well.
verses.kjv: And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.
verses.bsb: And wherever He went—villages and towns and countrysides—they laid the sick in the marketplaces and begged Him just to let them touch the fringe of His cloak. And all who touched Him were healed.

verses.esv: Now when the Pharisees gathered to him, with some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem,
verses.kjv: THEN came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees and some of the scribes who had come from Jerusalem gathered around Jesus,

verses.esv: they saw that some of his disciples ate with hands that were defiled, that is, unwashed.
verses.kjv: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.
verses.bsb: and they saw some of His disciples eating with hands that were defiled—that is, unwashed.

verses.esv: (For the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands properly, holding to the tradition of the elders,
verses.kjv: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.
verses.bsb: Now in holding to the tradition of the elders, the Pharisees and all the Jews do not eat until they wash their hands ceremonially.

verses.esv: and when they come from the marketplace, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other traditions that they observe, such as the washing of cups and pots and copper vessels and dining couches.)
verses.kjv: And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
verses.bsb: And on returning from the market, they do not eat unless they wash. And there are many other traditions for them to observe, including the washing of cups, pitchers, kettles, and couches for dining.

verses.esv: And the Pharisees and the scribes asked him, “Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat with defiled hands?”
verses.kjv: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?
verses.bsb: So the Pharisees and scribes questioned Jesus: “Why do Your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders? Instead, they eat with defiled hands.”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Well did Isaiah prophesy of you hypocrites, as it is written, “‘This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me;
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered them, “Isaiah prophesied correctly about you hypocrites, as it is written:  ‘These people honor Me with their lips, but their hearts are far from Me.

verses.esv: in vain do they worship me, teaching as doctrines the commandments of men.’
verses.kjv: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
verses.bsb: They worship Me in vain; they teach as doctrine the precepts of men.’

verses.esv: You leave the commandment of God and hold to the tradition of men.”
verses.kjv: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
verses.bsb: You have disregarded the commandment of God to keep the tradition of men.”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “You have a fine way of rejecting the commandment of God in order to establish your tradition!
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
verses.bsb: He went on to say, “You neatly set aside the command of God to maintain your own tradition.

verses.esv: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’; and, ‘Whoever reviles father or mother must surely die.’
verses.kjv: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:
verses.bsb: For Moses said, ‘Honor your father and your mother’ and ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’

verses.esv: But you say, ‘If a man tells his father or his mother, “Whatever you would have gained from me is Corban”’ (that is, given to God)—
verses.kjv: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.
verses.bsb: But you say that if a man says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever you would have received from me is Corban’ (that is, a gift devoted to God),

verses.esv: then you no longer permit him to do anything for his father or mother,
verses.kjv: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;
verses.bsb: he is no longer permitted to do anything for his father or mother.

verses.esv: thus making void the word of God by your tradition that you have handed down. And many such things you do.”
verses.kjv: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.
verses.bsb: Thus you nullify the word of God by the tradition you have handed down. And you do so in many such matters.”

verses.esv: And he called the people to him again and said to them, “Hear me, all of you, and understand:
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus called the crowd to Him and said, “All of you, listen to Me and understand:

verses.esv: There is nothing outside a person that by going into him can defile him, but the things that come out of a person are what defile him.”
verses.kjv: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.
verses.bsb: Nothing that enters a man from the outside can defile him; but the things that come out of a man, these are what defile him.”

verses.esv: And when he had entered the house and left the people, his disciples asked him about the parable.
verses.kjv: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
verses.bsb: After Jesus had left the crowd and gone into the house, His disciples inquired about the parable.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Then are you also without understanding? Do you not see that whatever goes into a person from outside cannot defile him,
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
verses.bsb: “Are you still so dull?” He asked. “Do you not understand? Nothing that enters a man from the outside can defile him,

verses.esv: since it enters not his heart but his stomach, and is expelled?” (Thus he declared all foods clean.)
verses.kjv: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
verses.bsb: because it does not enter his heart, but it goes into the stomach and then is eliminated.” (Thus all foods are clean.)

verses.esv: And he said, “What comes out of a person is what defiles him.
verses.kjv: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.
verses.bsb: He continued: “What comes out of a man, that is what defiles him.

verses.esv: For from within, out of the heart of man, come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,
verses.kjv: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,
verses.bsb: For from within the hearts of men come evil thoughts, sexual immorality, theft, murder, adultery,

verses.esv: coveting, wickedness, deceit, sensuality, envy, slander, pride, foolishness.
verses.kjv: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:
verses.bsb: greed, wickedness, deceit, debauchery, envy, slander, arrogance, and foolishness.

verses.esv: All these evil things come from within, and they defile a person.”
verses.kjv: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.
verses.bsb: All these evils come from within, and these are what defile a man.”

verses.esv: And from there he arose and went away to the region of Tyre and Sidon. And he entered a house and did not want anyone to know, yet he could not be hidden.
verses.kjv: ¶ And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.
verses.bsb: Jesus left that place and went to the region of Tyre. Not wanting anyone to know He was there, He entered a house, but was unable to escape their notice.

verses.esv: But immediately a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit heard of him and came and fell down at his feet.
verses.kjv: For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:
verses.bsb: Instead, a woman whose little daughter had an unclean spirit soon heard about Jesus, and she came and fell at His feet.

verses.esv: Now the woman was a Gentile, a Syrophoenician by birth. And she begged him to cast the demon out of her daughter.
verses.kjv: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
verses.bsb: Now she was a Greek woman of Syrophoenician origin, and she kept asking Jesus to drive the demon out of her daughter.

verses.esv: And he said to her, “Let the children be fed first, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.”
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.
verses.bsb: “First let the children have their fill,” He said. “For it is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to the dogs.”

verses.esv: But she answered him, “Yes, Lord; yet even the dogs under the table eat the children's crumbs.”
verses.kjv: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children’s crumbs.
verses.bsb: “Yes, Lord,” she replied, “even the dogs under the table eat the children’s crumbs.”

verses.esv: And he said to her, “For this statement you may go your way; the demon has left your daughter.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told her, “Because of this answer, you may go. The demon has left your daughter.”

verses.esv: And she went home and found the child lying in bed and the demon gone.
verses.kjv: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.
verses.bsb: And she went home and found her child lying on the bed, and the demon was gone.

verses.esv: Then he returned from the region of Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee, in the region of the Decapolis.
verses.kjv: ¶ And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus left the region of Tyre and went through Sidon to the Sea of Galilee and into the region of the Decapolis.

verses.esv: And they brought to him a man who was deaf and had a speech impediment, and they begged him to lay his hand on him.
verses.kjv: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.
verses.bsb: Some people brought to Him a man who was deaf and hardly able to speak, and they begged Jesus to place His hand on him.

verses.esv: And taking him aside from the crowd privately, he put his fingers into his ears, and after spitting touched his tongue.
verses.kjv: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
verses.bsb: So Jesus took him aside privately, away from the crowd, and put His fingers into the man’s ears. Then He spit and touched the man’s tongue.

verses.esv: And looking up to heaven, he sighed and said to him, “Ephphatha,” that is, “Be opened.”
verses.kjv: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
verses.bsb: And looking up to heaven, He sighed deeply and said to him, “Ephphatha!” (which means, “Be opened!”).

verses.esv: And his ears were opened, his tongue was released, and he spoke plainly.
verses.kjv: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.
verses.bsb: Immediately the man’s ears were opened and his tongue was released, and he began to speak plainly.

verses.esv: And Jesus charged them to tell no one. But the more he charged them, the more zealously they proclaimed it.
verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published it;
verses.bsb: Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone. But the more He ordered them, the more widely they proclaimed it.

verses.esv: And they were astonished beyond measure, saying, “He has done all things well. He even makes the deaf hear and the mute speak.”
verses.kjv: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.
verses.bsb: The people were utterly astonished and said, “He has done all things well! He makes even the deaf hear and the mute speak!”

verses.esv: In those days, when again a great crowd had gathered, and they had nothing to eat, he called his disciples to him and said to them,
verses.kjv: IN those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,
verses.bsb: In those days the crowd once again became very large, and they had nothing to eat. Jesus called the disciples to Him and said,

verses.esv: “I have compassion on the crowd, because they have been with me now three days and have nothing to eat.
verses.kjv: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
verses.bsb: “I have compassion for this crowd, because they have already been with Me three days and have nothing to eat.

verses.esv: And if I send them away hungry to their homes, they will faint on the way. And some of them have come from far away.”
verses.kjv: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.
verses.bsb: If I send them home hungry, they will faint along the way. For some of them have come a great distance.”

verses.esv: And his disciples answered him, “How can one feed these people with bread here in this desolate place?”
verses.kjv: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
verses.bsb: His disciples replied, “Where in this desolate place could anyone find enough bread to feed all these people?”

verses.esv: And he asked them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven.”
verses.kjv: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
verses.bsb: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked.  “Seven,” they replied.

verses.esv: And he directed the crowd to sit down on the ground. And he took the seven loaves, and having given thanks, he broke them and gave them to his disciples to set before the people; and they set them before the crowd.
verses.kjv: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.
verses.bsb: And He instructed the crowd to sit down on the ground. Then He took the seven loaves, gave thanks and broke them, and gave them to His disciples to set before the people. And they distributed them to the crowd.

verses.esv: And they had a few small fish. And having blessed them, he said that these also should be set before them.
verses.kjv: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.
verses.bsb: They also had a few small fish, and Jesus blessed them and ordered that these be set before them as well.

verses.esv: And they ate and were satisfied. And they took up the broken pieces left over, seven baskets full.
verses.kjv: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.
verses.bsb: The people ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.

verses.esv: And there were about four thousand people. And he sent them away.
verses.kjv: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
verses.bsb: And about four thousand men were present.  As soon as Jesus had dismissed the crowd,

verses.esv: And immediately he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.
verses.kjv: ¶ And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
verses.bsb: He got into the boat with His disciples and went to the district of Dalmanutha.

verses.esv: The Pharisees came and began to argue with him, seeking from him a sign from heaven to test him.
verses.kjv: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.
verses.bsb: Then the Pharisees came and began to argue with Jesus, testing Him by demanding from Him a sign from heaven.

verses.esv: And he sighed deeply in his spirit and said, “Why does this generation seek a sign? Truly, I say to you, no sign will be given to this generation.”
verses.kjv: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
verses.bsb: Jesus sighed deeply in His spirit and said, “Why does this generation demand a sign? Truly I tell you, no sign will be given to this generation.”

verses.esv: And he left them, got into the boat again, and went to the other side.
verses.kjv: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
verses.bsb: And He left them, got back into the boat, and crossed to the other side.

verses.esv: Now they had forgotten to bring bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
verses.bsb: Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, except for one loaf they had with them in the boat.

verses.esv: And he cautioned them, saying, “Watch out; beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod.”
verses.kjv: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
verses.bsb: “Watch out!” He cautioned them. “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of Herod.”

verses.esv: And they began discussing with one another the fact that they had no bread.
verses.kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.
verses.bsb: So they began to discuss with one another the fact that they had no bread.

verses.esv: And Jesus, aware of this, said to them, “Why are you discussing the fact that you have no bread? Do you not yet perceive or understand? Are your hearts hardened?
verses.kjv: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?
verses.bsb: Aware of their conversation, Jesus asked them, “Why are you debating about having no bread? Do you still not see or understand? Do you have such hard hearts?

verses.esv: Having eyes do you not see, and having ears do you not hear? And do you not remember?
verses.kjv: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
verses.bsb: ‘Having eyes, do you not see? And having ears, do you not hear?’ And do you not remember?

verses.esv: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?” They said to him, “Twelve.”
verses.kjv: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
verses.bsb: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?”  “Twelve,” they answered.

verses.esv: “And the seven for the four thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up?” And they said to him, “Seven.”
verses.kjv: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
verses.bsb: “And when I broke the seven loaves for the four thousand, how many basketfuls of broken pieces did you collect?”  “Seven,” they said.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Do you not yet understand?”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
verses.bsb: Then He asked them, “Do you still not understand?”

verses.esv: And they came to Bethsaida. And some people brought to him a blind man and begged him to touch him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.
verses.bsb: When they arrived at Bethsaida, some people brought a blind man and begged Jesus to touch him.

verses.esv: And he took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village, and when he had spit on his eyes and laid his hands on him, he asked him, “Do you see anything?”
verses.kjv: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.
verses.bsb: So He took the blind man by the hand and led him out of the village. Then He spit on the man’s eyes and placed His hands on him. “Can you see anything?” He asked.

verses.esv: And he looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees, walking.”
verses.kjv: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
verses.bsb: The man looked up and said, “I can see the people, but they look like trees walking around.”

verses.esv: Then Jesus laid his hands on his eyes again; and he opened his eyes, his sight was restored, and he saw everything clearly.
verses.kjv: After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.
verses.bsb: Once again Jesus placed His hands on the man’s eyes, and when he opened them his sight was restored, and he could see everything clearly.

verses.esv: And he sent him to his home, saying, “Do not even enter the village.”
verses.kjv: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
verses.bsb: Jesus sent him home and said, “Do not go back into the village.”

verses.esv: And Jesus went on with his disciples to the villages of Caesarea Philippi. And on the way he asked his disciples, “Who do people say that I am?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus and His disciples went on to the villages around Caesarea Philippi. On the way, He questioned His disciples: “Who do people say I am?”

verses.esv: And they told him, “John the Baptist; and others say, Elijah; and others, one of the prophets.”
verses.kjv: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
verses.bsb: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, one of the prophets.”

verses.esv: And he asked them, “But who do you say that I am?” Peter answered him, “You are the Christ.”
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.
verses.bsb: “But what about you?” Jesus asked. “Who do you say I am?”  Peter answered, “You are the Christ.”

verses.esv: And he strictly charged them to tell no one about him.
verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.
verses.bsb: And Jesus warned them not to tell anyone about Him.

verses.esv: And he began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders and the chief priests and the scribes and be killed, and after three days rise again.
verses.kjv: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
verses.bsb: Then He began to teach them that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and that He must be killed and after three days rise again.

verses.esv: And he said this plainly. And Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him.
verses.kjv: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.
verses.bsb: He spoke this message quite frankly, and Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him.

verses.esv: But turning and seeing his disciples, he rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind me, Satan! For you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of man.”
verses.kjv: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.
verses.bsb: But Jesus, turning and looking at His disciples, rebuked Peter and said, “Get behind Me, Satan! For you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.”

verses.esv: And calling the crowd to him with his disciples, he said to them, “If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus called the crowd to Him along with His disciples, and He told them, “If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.

verses.esv: For whoever would save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for my sake and the gospel's will save it.
verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
verses.bsb: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and for the gospel will save it.

verses.esv: For what does it profit a man to gain the whole world and forfeit his soul?
verses.kjv: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
verses.bsb: What does it profit a man to gain the whole world, yet forfeit his soul?

verses.esv: For what can a man give in return for his soul?
verses.kjv: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?
verses.bsb: Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?

verses.esv: For whoever is ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of him will the Son of Man also be ashamed when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.” And he said to them, “Truly, I say to you, there are some standing here who will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God after it has come with power.”
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
verses.bsb: If anyone is ashamed of Me and My words in this adulterous and sinful generation, the Son of Man will also be ashamed of him when He comes in His Father’s glory with the holy angels.”

verses.esv: And after six days Jesus took with him Peter and James and John, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. And he was transfigured before them,
verses.kjv: ¶ And after six days Jesus taketh with him Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was transfigured before them.
verses.bsb: After six days Jesus took with Him Peter, James, and John, and led them up a high mountain by themselves. There He was transfigured before them.

verses.esv: and his clothes became radiant, intensely white, as no one on earth could bleach them.
verses.kjv: And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.
verses.bsb: His clothes became radiantly white, brighter than any launderer on earth could bleach them.

verses.esv: And there appeared to them Elijah with Moses, and they were talking with Jesus.
verses.kjv: And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.
verses.bsb: And Elijah and Moses appeared before them, talking with Jesus.

verses.esv: And Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good that we are here. Let us make three tents, one for you and one for Moses and one for Elijah.”
verses.kjv: And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
verses.bsb: Peter said to Jesus, “Rabbi, it is good for us to be here. Let us put up three shelters—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”

verses.esv: For he did not know what to say, for they were terrified.
verses.kjv: For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.
verses.bsb: For they were all so terrified that Peter did not know what else to say.

verses.esv: And a cloud overshadowed them, and a voice came out of the cloud, “This is my beloved Son; listen to him.”
verses.kjv: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.
verses.bsb: Then a cloud appeared and enveloped them, and a voice came from the cloud: “This is My beloved Son. Listen to Him!”

verses.esv: And suddenly, looking around, they no longer saw anyone with them but Jesus only.
verses.kjv: And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.
verses.bsb: Suddenly, when they looked around, they saw no one with them except Jesus.

verses.esv: And as they were coming down the mountain, he charged them to tell no one what they had seen, until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
verses.kjv: And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.
verses.bsb: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus admonished them not to tell anyone what they had seen until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.

verses.esv: So they kept the matter to themselves, questioning what this rising from the dead might mean.
verses.kjv: And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean.
verses.bsb: So they kept this matter to themselves, discussing what it meant to rise from the dead.

verses.esv: And they asked him, “Why do the scribes say that first Elijah must come?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?
verses.bsb: And they asked Jesus, “Why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Elijah does come first to restore all things. And how is it written of the Son of Man that he should suffer many things and be treated with contempt?
verses.kjv: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.
verses.bsb: He replied, “Elijah does indeed come first, and he restores all things. Why then is it written that the Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected?

verses.esv: But I tell you that Elijah has come, and they did to him whatever they pleased, as it is written of him.”
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.
verses.bsb: But I tell you that Elijah has indeed come, and they have done to him whatever they wished, just as it is written about him.”

verses.esv: And when they came to the disciples, they saw a great crowd around them, and scribes arguing with them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.
verses.bsb: When they returned to the other disciples, they saw a large crowd around them, and scribes arguing with them.

verses.esv: And immediately all the crowd, when they saw him, were greatly amazed and ran up to him and greeted him.
verses.kjv: And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to him saluted him.
verses.bsb: As soon as all the people saw Jesus, they were filled with awe and ran to greet Him.

verses.esv: And he asked them, “What are you arguing about with them?”
verses.kjv: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?
verses.bsb: “What are you disputing with them?” He asked.

verses.esv: And someone from the crowd answered him, “Teacher, I brought my son to you, for he has a spirit that makes him mute.
verses.kjv: And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;
verses.bsb: Someone in the crowd replied, “Teacher, I brought You my son, who has a spirit that makes him mute.

verses.esv: And whenever it seizes him, it throws him down, and he foams and grinds his teeth and becomes rigid. So I asked your disciples to cast it out, and they were not able.”
verses.kjv: And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
verses.bsb: Whenever it seizes him, it throws him to the ground. He foams at the mouth, gnashes his teeth, and becomes rigid. I asked Your disciples to drive it out, but they were unable.”

verses.esv: And he answered them, “O faithless generation, how long am I to be with you? How long am I to bear with you? Bring him to me.”
verses.kjv: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
verses.bsb: “O unbelieving generation!” Jesus replied. “How long must I remain with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy to Me.”

verses.esv: And they brought the boy to him. And when the spirit saw him, immediately it convulsed the boy, and he fell on the ground and rolled about, foaming at the mouth.
verses.kjv: And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.
verses.bsb: So they brought him, and seeing Jesus, the spirit immediately threw the boy into a convulsion. He fell to the ground and rolled around, foaming at the mouth.

verses.esv: And Jesus asked his father, “How long has this been happening to him?” And he said, “From childhood.
verses.kjv: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
verses.bsb: Jesus asked the boy’s father, “How long has this been with him?”  “From childhood,” he said.

verses.esv: And it has often cast him into fire and into water, to destroy him. But if you can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.”
verses.kjv: And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.
verses.bsb: “It often throws him into the fire or into the water, trying to kill him. But if You can do anything, have compassion on us and help us.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “‘If you can’! All things are possible for one who believes.”
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.
verses.bsb: “If You can?” echoed Jesus. “All things are possible to him who believes!”

verses.esv: Immediately the father of the child cried out and said, “I believe; help my unbelief!”
verses.kjv: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.
verses.bsb: Immediately the boy’s father cried out, “I do believe; help my unbelief!”

verses.esv: And when Jesus saw that a crowd came running together, he rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to it, “You mute and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him and never enter him again.”
verses.kjv: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw that a crowd had come running, He rebuked the unclean spirit. “You deaf and mute spirit,” He said, “I command you to come out and never enter him again.”

verses.esv: And after crying out and convulsing him terribly, it came out, and the boy was like a corpse, so that most of them said, “He is dead.”
verses.kjv: And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.
verses.bsb: After shrieking and convulsing him violently, the spirit came out. The boy became like a corpse, so that many said, “He is dead.”

verses.esv: But Jesus took him by the hand and lifted him up, and he arose.
verses.kjv: But Jesus took him by the hand, and lifted him up; and he arose.
verses.bsb: But Jesus took him by the hand and helped him to his feet, and he stood up.

verses.esv: And when he had entered the house, his disciples asked him privately, “Why could we not cast it out?”
verses.kjv: And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?
verses.bsb: After Jesus had gone into the house, His disciples asked Him privately, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “This kind cannot be driven out by anything but prayer.”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.
verses.bsb: Jesus answered, “This kind cannot come out, except by prayer.”

verses.esv: They went on from there and passed through Galilee. And he did not want anyone to know,
verses.kjv: ¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.
verses.bsb: Going on from there, they passed through Galilee. But Jesus did not want anyone to know,

verses.esv: for he was teaching his disciples, saying to them, “The Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men, and they will kill him. And when he is killed, after three days he will rise.”
verses.kjv: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.
verses.bsb: because He was teaching His disciples. He told them, “The Son of Man will be delivered into the hands of men. They will kill Him, and after three days He will rise.”

verses.esv: But they did not understand the saying, and were afraid to ask him.
verses.kjv: But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.
verses.bsb: But they did not understand this statement, and they were afraid to ask Him about it.

verses.esv: And they came to Capernaum. And when he was in the house he asked them, “What were you discussing on the way?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?
verses.bsb: Then they came to Capernaum. While Jesus was in the house, He asked them, “What were you discussing on the way?”

verses.esv: But they kept silent, for on the way they had argued with one another about who was the greatest.
verses.kjv: But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who should be the greatest.
verses.bsb: But they were silent, for on the way they had been arguing about which of them was the greatest.

verses.esv: And he sat down and called the twelve. And he said to them, “If anyone would be first, he must be last of all and servant of all.”
verses.kjv: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.
verses.bsb: Sitting down, Jesus called the Twelve and said, “If anyone wants to be first, he must be the last of all and the servant of all.”

verses.esv: And he took a child and put him in the midst of them, and taking him in his arms, he said to them,
verses.kjv: And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,
verses.bsb: Then He had a little child stand among them. Taking the child in His arms, He said to them,

verses.esv: “Whoever receives one such child in my name receives me, and whoever receives me, receives not me but him who sent me.”
verses.kjv: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
verses.bsb: “Whoever welcomes one of these little children in My name welcomes Me, and whoever welcomes Me welcomes not only Me, but the One who sent Me.”

verses.esv: John said to him, “Teacher, we saw someone casting out demons in your name, and we tried to stop him, because he was not following us.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
verses.bsb: John said to Him, “Teacher, we saw someone else driving out demons in Your name, and we tried to stop him, because he does not accompany us.”

verses.esv: But Jesus said, “Do not stop him, for no one who does a mighty work in my name will be able soon afterward to speak evil of me.
verses.kjv: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
verses.bsb: “Do not stop him,” Jesus replied. “For no one who performs a miracle in My name can turn around and speak evil of Me.

verses.esv: For the one who is not against us is for us.
verses.kjv: For he that is not against us is on our part.
verses.bsb: For whoever is not against us is for us.

verses.esv: For truly, I say to you, whoever gives you a cup of water to drink because you belong to Christ will by no means lose his reward.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.
verses.bsb: Indeed, if anyone gives you even a cup of water because you bear the name of Christ, truly I tell you, he will never lose his reward.

verses.esv: “Whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to sin, it would be better for him if a great millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.
verses.bsb: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be thrown into the sea.

verses.esv: And if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life crippled than with two hands to go to hell, to the unquenchable fire.
verses.kjv: And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
verses.bsb: If your hand causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life crippled than to have two hands and go into hell, into the unquenchable fire.

verses.esv: And if your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life lame than with two feet to be thrown into hell.
verses.kjv: And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
verses.bsb: If your foot causes you to sin, cut it off. It is better for you to enter life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.

verses.esv: And if your eye causes you to sin, tear it out. It is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than with two eyes to be thrown into hell,
verses.kjv: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:
verses.bsb: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out. It is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell,

verses.esv: ‘where their worm does not die and the fire is not quenched.’
verses.kjv: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
verses.bsb: where ‘their worm never dies, and the fire is never quenched.’

verses.esv: For everyone will be salted with fire.
verses.kjv: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
verses.bsb: For everyone will be salted with fire.

verses.esv: Salt is good, but if the salt has lost its saltiness, how will you make it salty again? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”
verses.kjv: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.
verses.bsb: Salt is good, but if the salt loses its saltiness, with what will you season it? Have salt among yourselves, and be at peace with one another.”

verses.esv: And he left there and went to the region of Judea and beyond the Jordan, and crowds gathered to him again. And again, as was his custom, he taught them.
verses.kjv: AND he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judæa by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus left that place and went into the region of Judea, beyond the Jordan. Again the crowds came to Him and He taught them, as was His custom.

verses.esv: And Pharisees came up and in order to test him asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
verses.bsb: Some Pharisees came to test Him. “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife?” they inquired.

verses.esv: He answered them, “What did Moses command you?”
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
verses.bsb: “What did Moses command you?” He replied.

verses.esv: They said, “Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of divorce and to send her away.”
verses.kjv: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.
verses.bsb: They answered, “Moses permitted a man to write his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to them, “Because of your hardness of heart he wrote you this commandment.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
verses.bsb: But Jesus told them, “Moses wrote this commandment for you because of your hardness of heart.

verses.esv: But from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.’
verses.kjv: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
verses.bsb: However, from the beginning of creation, ‘God made them male and female.’

verses.esv: ‘Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and hold fast to his wife,
verses.kjv: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
verses.bsb: ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife,

verses.esv: and the two shall become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two but one flesh.
verses.kjv: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
verses.bsb: and the two will become one flesh.’ So they are no longer two, but one flesh.

verses.esv: What therefore God has joined together, let not man separate.”
verses.kjv: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.
verses.bsb: Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate.”

verses.esv: And in the house the disciples asked him again about this matter.
verses.kjv: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
verses.bsb: When they were back inside the house, the disciples asked Jesus about this matter.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her,
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
verses.bsb: So He told them, “Whoever divorces his wife and marries another woman commits adultery against her.

verses.esv: and if she divorces her husband and marries another, she commits adultery.”
verses.kjv: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
verses.bsb: And if a woman divorces her husband and marries another man, she commits adultery.”

verses.esv: And they were bringing children to him that he might touch them, and the disciples rebuked them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
verses.bsb: Now people were bringing the little children to Jesus for Him to place His hands on them, and the disciples rebuked those who brought them.

verses.esv: But when Jesus saw it, he was indignant and said to them, “Let the children come to me; do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: But when Jesus saw this, He was indignant and told them, “Let the little children come to Me, and do not hinder them! For the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child shall not enter it.”
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, anyone who does not receive the kingdom of God like a little child will never enter it.”

verses.esv: And he took them in his arms and blessed them, laying his hands on them.
verses.kjv: And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.
verses.bsb: And He took the children in His arms, placed His hands on them, and blessed them.

verses.esv: And as he was setting out on his journey, a man ran up and knelt before him and asked him, “Good Teacher, what must I do to inherit eternal life?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
verses.bsb: As Jesus started on His way, a man ran up and knelt before Him. “Good Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to inherit eternal life?”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Why do you call me good? No one is good except God alone.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.
verses.bsb: “Why do you call Me good?” Jesus replied. “No one is good except God alone.

verses.esv: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder, Do not commit adultery, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honor your father and mother.’”
verses.kjv: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.
verses.bsb: You know the commandments: ‘Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not bear false witness, do not cheat others, honor your father and mother.’”

verses.esv: And he said to him, “Teacher, all these I have kept from my youth.”
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” he replied, “all these I have kept from my youth.”

verses.esv: And Jesus, looking at him, loved him, and said to him, “You lack one thing: go, sell all that you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”
verses.kjv: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.
verses.bsb: Jesus looked at him, loved him, and said to him, “There is one thing you lack: Go, sell everything you own and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow Me.”

verses.esv: Disheartened by the saying, he went away sorrowful, for he had great possessions.
verses.kjv: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
verses.bsb: But the man was saddened by these words and went away in sorrow, because he had great wealth.

verses.esv: And Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “How difficult it will be for those who have wealth to enter the kingdom of God!”
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!
verses.bsb: Then Jesus looked around and said to His disciples, “How hard it is for the rich to enter the kingdom of God!”

verses.esv: And the disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus said to them again, “Children, how difficult it is to enter the kingdom of God!
verses.kjv: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
verses.bsb: And the disciples were amazed at His words.  But Jesus said to them again, “Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God!

verses.esv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
verses.kjv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: It is easier for a camel to pass through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”

verses.esv: And they were exceedingly astonished, and said to him, “Then who can be saved?”
verses.kjv: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?
verses.bsb: They were even more astonished and said to one another, “Who then can be saved?”

verses.esv: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man it is impossible, but not with God. For all things are possible with God.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.
verses.bsb: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but not with God. For all things are possible with God.”

verses.esv: Peter began to say to him, “See, we have left everything and followed you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
verses.bsb: Peter began to say to Him, “Look, we have left everything and followed You.”

verses.esv: Jesus said, “Truly, I say to you, there is no one who has left house or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or lands, for my sake and for the gospel,
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel’s,
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus replied, “no one who has left home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for My sake and for the gospel

verses.esv: who will not receive a hundredfold now in this time, houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and lands, with persecutions, and in the age to come eternal life.
verses.kjv: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.
verses.bsb: will fail to receive a hundredfold in the present age—houses and brothers and sisters and mothers and children and fields, along with persecutions—and in the age to come, eternal life.

verses.esv: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.”
verses.kjv: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.
verses.bsb: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.”

verses.esv: And they were on the road, going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was walking ahead of them. And they were amazed, and those who followed were afraid. And taking the twelve again, he began to tell them what was to happen to him,
verses.kjv: ¶ And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,
verses.bsb: As they were going up the road to Jerusalem, Jesus was walking ahead of them. The disciples were amazed, but those who followed were afraid. Again Jesus took the Twelve aside and began to tell them what was going to happen to Him:

verses.esv: saying, “See, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and the scribes, and they will condemn him to death and deliver him over to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
verses.bsb: “Look, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered over to the chief priests and scribes. They will condemn Him to death and will deliver Him over to the Gentiles,

verses.esv: And they will mock him and spit on him, and flog him and kill him. And after three days he will rise.”
verses.kjv: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.
verses.bsb: who will mock Him and spit on Him and flog Him and kill Him. And after three days He will rise again.”

verses.esv: And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came up to him and said to him, “Teacher, we want you to do for us whatever we ask of you.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.
verses.bsb: Then James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Jesus and declared, “Teacher, we want You to do for us whatever we ask.”

verses.esv: And he said to them, “What do you want me to do for you?”
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
verses.bsb: “What do you want Me to do for you?” He inquired.

verses.esv: And they said to him, “Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory.”
verses.kjv: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
verses.bsb: They answered, “Grant that one of us may sit at Your right hand and the other at Your left in Your glory.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “You do not know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I drink, or to be baptized with the baptism with which I am baptized?”
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
verses.bsb: “You do not know what you are asking,” Jesus replied. “Can you drink the cup I will drink, or be baptized with the baptism I will undergo?”

verses.esv: And they said to him, “We are able.” And Jesus said to them, “The cup that I drink you will drink, and with the baptism with which I am baptized, you will be baptized,
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
verses.bsb: “We can,” the brothers answered.  “You will drink the cup that I drink,” Jesus said, “and you will be baptized with the baptism that I undergo.

verses.esv: but to sit at my right hand or at my left is not mine to grant, but it is for those for whom it has been prepared.”
verses.kjv: But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.
verses.bsb: But to sit at My right or left is not Mine to grant. These seats belong to those for whom they have been prepared.”

verses.esv: And when the ten heard it, they began to be indignant at James and John.
verses.kjv: And when the ten heard it, they began to be much displeased with James and John.
verses.bsb: When the ten heard about this, they became indignant with James and John.

verses.esv: And Jesus called them to him and said to them, “You know that those who are considered rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.
verses.kjv: But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.
verses.bsb: So Jesus called them together and said, “You know that those regarded as rulers of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their superiors exercise authority over them.

verses.esv: But it shall not be so among you. But whoever would be great among you must be your servant,
verses.kjv: But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:
verses.bsb: But it shall not be this way among you. Instead, whoever wants to become great among you must be your servant,

verses.esv: and whoever would be first among you must be slave of all.
verses.kjv: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.
verses.bsb: and whoever wants to be first must be the slave of all.

verses.esv: For even the Son of Man came not to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”
verses.kjv: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.
verses.bsb: For even the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many.”

verses.esv: And they came to Jericho. And as he was leaving Jericho with his disciples and a great crowd, Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, the son of Timaeus, was sitting by the roadside.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Bartimæus, the son of Timæus, sat by the highway side begging.
verses.bsb: Next, they came to Jericho. And as Jesus and His disciples were leaving Jericho with a large crowd, a blind beggar named Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, was sitting beside the road.

verses.esv: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out and say, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
verses.kjv: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.bsb: When he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, “Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!”

verses.esv: And many rebuked him, telling him to be silent. But he cried out all the more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
verses.kjv: And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
verses.bsb: Many people admonished him to be silent, but he cried out all the louder, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”

verses.esv: And Jesus stopped and said, “Call him.” And they called the blind man, saying to him, “Take heart. Get up; he is calling you.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
verses.bsb: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him.”  So they called the blind man. “Take courage!” they said. “Get up! He is calling for you.”

verses.esv: And throwing off his cloak, he sprang up and came to Jesus.
verses.kjv: And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.
verses.bsb: Throwing off his cloak, Bartimaeus jumped up and came to Jesus.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” And the blind man said to him, “Rabbi, let me recover my sight.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
verses.bsb: “What do you want Me to do for you?” Jesus asked.  “Rabboni,” said the blind man, “let me see again.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Go your way; your faith has made you well.” And immediately he recovered his sight and followed him on the way.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.
verses.bsb: “Go,” said Jesus, “your faith has healed you.” And immediately he received his sight and followed Jesus along the road.

verses.esv: Now when they drew near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent two of his disciples
verses.kjv: AND when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,
verses.bsb: As they approached Jerusalem and came to Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, Jesus sent out two of His disciples

verses.esv: and said to them, “Go into the village in front of you, and immediately as you enter it you will find a colt tied, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it.
verses.kjv: And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.
verses.bsb: and said to them, “Go into the village ahead of you, and as soon as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.

verses.esv: If anyone says to you, ‘Why are you doing this?’ say, ‘The Lord has need of it and will send it back here immediately.’”
verses.kjv: And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.
verses.bsb: If anyone asks, ‘Why are you doing this?’ tell him, ‘The Lord needs it and will return it shortly.’”

verses.esv: And they went away and found a colt tied at a door outside in the street, and they untied it.
verses.kjv: And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him.
verses.bsb: So they went and found the colt outside in the street, tied at a doorway. They untied it,

verses.esv: And some of those standing there said to them, “What are you doing, untying the colt?”
verses.kjv: And certain of them that stood there said unto them, What do ye, loosing the colt?
verses.bsb: and some who were standing there asked, “Why are you untying the colt?”

verses.esv: And they told them what Jesus had said, and they let them go.
verses.kjv: And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.
verses.bsb: The disciples answered as Jesus had instructed them, and the people gave them permission.

verses.esv: And they brought the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks on it, and he sat on it.
verses.kjv: And they brought the colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him.
verses.bsb: Then they led the colt to Jesus and threw their cloaks over it, and He sat on it.

verses.esv: And many spread their cloaks on the road, and others spread leafy branches that they had cut from the fields.
verses.kjv: And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way.
verses.bsb: Many in the crowd spread their cloaks on the road, while others spread branches they had cut from the fields.

verses.esv: And those who went before and those who followed were shouting, “Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!
