verses.kjv: And they that went before, and they that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord:
verses.bsb: The ones who went ahead and those who followed were shouting:  “Hosanna!”   “Blessed is He who comes in the name of the Lord!”

verses.esv: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David! Hosanna in the highest!”
verses.kjv: Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.
verses.bsb: “Blessed is the coming kingdom of our father David!”   “Hosanna in the highest!”

verses.esv: And he entered Jerusalem and went into the temple. And when he had looked around at everything, as it was already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
verses.kjv: And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus entered Jerusalem and went into the temple courts. He looked around at everything, but since it was already late, He went out to Bethany with the Twelve.

verses.esv: On the following day, when they came from Bethany, he was hungry.
verses.kjv: ¶ And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry:
verses.bsb: The next day, when they had left Bethany, Jesus was hungry.

verses.esv: And seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, he went to see if he could find anything on it. When he came to it, he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
verses.kjv: And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not yet.
verses.bsb: Seeing in the distance a fig tree in leaf, He went to see if there was any fruit on it. But when He reached it, He found nothing on it except leaves, since it was not the season for figs.

verses.esv: And he said to it, “May no one ever eat fruit from you again.” And his disciples heard it.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard it.
verses.bsb: Then He said to the tree, “May no one ever eat of your fruit again.” And His disciples heard this statement.

verses.esv: And they came to Jerusalem. And he entered the temple and began to drive out those who sold and those who bought in the temple, and he overturned the tables of the money-changers and the seats of those who sold pigeons.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves;
verses.bsb: When they arrived in Jerusalem, Jesus entered the temple courts and began to drive out those who were buying and selling there. He overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those selling doves.

verses.esv: And he would not allow anyone to carry anything through the temple.
verses.kjv: And would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple.
verses.bsb: And He would not allow anyone to carry merchandise through the temple courts.

verses.esv: And he was teaching them and saying to them, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it a den of robbers.”
verses.kjv: And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus began to teach them, and He declared, “Is it not written: ‘My house will be called a house of prayer for all the nations’? But you have made it ‘a den of robbers.’”

verses.esv: And the chief priests and the scribes heard it and were seeking a way to destroy him, for they feared him, because all the crowd was astonished at his teaching.
verses.kjv: And the scribes and chief priests heard it, and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.
verses.bsb: When the chief priests and scribes heard this, they looked for a way to kill Him. For they were afraid of Him, because the whole crowd was astonished at His teaching.

verses.esv: And when evening came they went out of the city.
verses.kjv: And when even was come, he went out of the city.
verses.bsb: And when evening came, Jesus and His disciples went out of the city.

verses.esv: As they passed by in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered away to its roots.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
verses.bsb: As they were walking back in the morning, they saw the fig tree withered from its roots.

verses.esv: And Peter remembered and said to him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree that you cursed has withered.”
verses.kjv: And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.
verses.bsb: Peter remembered it and said, “Look, Rabbi! The fig tree You cursed has withered.”

verses.esv: And Jesus answered them, “Have faith in God.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have faith in God.
verses.bsb: “Have faith in God,” Jesus said to them.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be taken up and thrown into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that what he says will come to pass, it will be done for him.
verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you that if anyone says to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ and has no doubt in his heart but believes that it will happen, it will be done for him.

verses.esv: Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
verses.kjv: Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.
verses.bsb: Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask for in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.

verses.esv: And whenever you stand praying, forgive, if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father also who is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.”
verses.kjv: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.
verses.bsb: And when you stand to pray, if you hold anything against another, forgive it, so that your Father in heaven will forgive your trespasses as well.”

verses.esv: And they came again to Jerusalem. And as he was walking in the temple, the chief priests and the scribes and the elders came to him,
verses.kjv: ¶ And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
verses.bsb: After their return to Jerusalem, Jesus was walking in the temple courts, and the chief priests, scribes, and elders came up to Him.

verses.esv: and they said to him, “By what authority are you doing these things, or who gave you this authority to do them?”
verses.kjv: And say unto him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
verses.bsb: “By what authority are You doing these things?” they asked. “And who gave You the authority to do them?”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “I will ask you one question; answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.bsb: “I will ask you one question,” Jesus replied, “and if you answer Me, I will tell you by what authority I am doing these things.

verses.esv: Was the baptism of John from heaven or from man? Answer me.”
verses.kjv: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.
verses.bsb: John’s baptism—was it from heaven or from men? Answer Me!”

verses.esv: And they discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’
verses.kjv: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
verses.bsb: They deliberated among themselves what they should answer: “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ He will ask, ‘Why then did you not believe him?’

verses.esv: But shall we say, ‘From man’?”—they were afraid of the people, for they all held that John really was a prophet.
verses.kjv: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.
verses.bsb: But if we say, ‘From men’...” they were afraid of the people, for they all held that John truly was a prophet.

verses.esv: So they answered Jesus, “We do not know.” And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
verses.kjv: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
verses.bsb: So they answered, “We do not know.”  And Jesus replied, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”

verses.esv: And he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a pit for the winepress and built a tower, and leased it to tenants and went into another country.
verses.kjv: AND he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a wine vat, and built a watchtower. Then he rented it out to some tenants and went away on a journey.

verses.esv: When the season came, he sent a servant to the tenants to get from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
verses.kjv: And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.
verses.bsb: At harvest time, he sent a servant to the tenants to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard.

verses.esv: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
verses.kjv: And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.
verses.bsb: But they seized the servant, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.

verses.esv: Again he sent to them another servant, and they struck him on the head and treated him shamefully.
verses.kjv: And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
verses.bsb: Then he sent them another servant, and they struck him over the head and treated him shamefully.

verses.esv: And he sent another, and him they killed. And so with many others: some they beat, and some they killed.
verses.kjv: And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.
verses.bsb: He sent still another, and this one they killed.  He sent many others; some they beat and others they killed.

verses.esv: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
verses.kjv: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
verses.bsb: Finally, having one beloved son, he sent him to them. ‘They will respect my son,’ he said.

verses.esv: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
verses.kjv: But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.
verses.bsb: But the tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’

verses.esv: And they took him and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.
verses.kjv: And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
verses.bsb: So they seized the son, killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.

verses.esv: What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
verses.kjv: What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.
verses.bsb: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those tenants, and will give the vineyard to others.

verses.esv: Have you not read this Scripture: “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
verses.kjv: And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:
verses.bsb: Have you never read this Scripture:  ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.

verses.esv: this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
verses.kjv: This was the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
verses.bsb: This is from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”

verses.esv: And they were seeking to arrest him but feared the people, for they perceived that he had told the parable against them. So they left him and went away.
verses.kjv: And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
verses.bsb: At this, the leaders sought to arrest Jesus, for they knew that He had spoken this parable against them. But fearing the crowd, they left Him and went away.

verses.esv: And they sent to him some of the Pharisees and some of the Herodians, to trap him in his talk.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.
verses.bsb: Later, they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to catch Jesus in His words.

verses.esv: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are true and do not care about anyone's opinion. For you are not swayed by appearances, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Should we pay them, or should we not?”
verses.kjv: And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?
verses.bsb: “Teacher,” they said, “we know that You are honest and seek favor from no one. Indeed, You are impartial and teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay them or not?”

verses.esv: But, knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why put me to the test? Bring me a denarius and let me look at it.”
verses.kjv: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.
verses.bsb: But Jesus saw through their hypocrisy and said, “Why are you testing Me? Bring Me a denarius to inspect.”

verses.esv: And they brought one. And he said to them, “Whose likeness and inscription is this?” They said to him, “Caesar's.”
verses.kjv: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Cæsar’s.
verses.bsb: So they brought it, and He asked them, “Whose image is this? And whose inscription?”  “Caesar’s,” they answered.

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.” And they marveled at him.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and to God the things that are God’s. And they marvelled at him.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus told them, “Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”  And they marveled at Him.

verses.esv: And Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection. And they asked him a question, saying,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus and questioned Him:

verses.esv: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife, but leaves no child, the man must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
verses.kjv: Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man’s brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
verses.bsb: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man is to marry his brother’s widow and raise up offspring for him.

verses.esv: There were seven brothers; the first took a wife, and when he died left no offspring.
verses.kjv: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.
verses.bsb: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died, leaving no children.

verses.esv: And the second took her, and died, leaving no offspring. And the third likewise.
verses.kjv: And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.
verses.bsb: Then the second one married the widow, but he also died and left no children. And the third did likewise.

verses.esv: And the seven left no offspring. Last of all the woman also died.
verses.kjv: And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.
verses.bsb: In this way, none of the seven left any children. And last of all, the woman died.

verses.esv: In the resurrection, when they rise again, whose wife will she be? For the seven had her as wife.”
verses.kjv: In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.
verses.bsb: In the resurrection, then, whose wife will she be? For all seven were married to her.”

verses.esv: Jesus said to them, “Is this not the reason you are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God?
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?
verses.bsb: Jesus said to them, “Aren’t you mistaken, because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God?

verses.esv: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
verses.kjv: For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.
verses.bsb: When the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage. Instead, they will be like the angels in heaven.

verses.esv: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
verses.kjv: And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?
verses.bsb: But concerning the dead rising, have you not read about the burning bush in the Book of Moses, how God told him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?

verses.esv: He is not God of the dead, but of the living. You are quite wrong.”
verses.kjv: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.
verses.bsb: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken!”

verses.esv: And one of the scribes came up and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him, Which is the first commandment of all?
verses.bsb: Now one of the scribes had come up and heard their debate. Noticing how well Jesus had answered them, he asked Him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?”

verses.esv: Jesus answered, “The most important is, ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:
verses.bsb: Jesus replied, “This is the most important: ‘Hear O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is One.

verses.esv: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.’
verses.kjv: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.
verses.bsb: Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.’

verses.esv: The second is this: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
verses.kjv: And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.
verses.bsb: The second is this: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’ No other commandment is greater than these.”

verses.esv: And the scribe said to him, “You are right, Teacher. You have truly said that he is one, and there is no other besides him.
verses.kjv: And the scribe said unto him, Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he:
verses.bsb: “Right, Teacher,” the scribe replied. “You have stated correctly that God is One and there is no other but Him,

verses.esv: And to love him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength, and to love one's neighbor as oneself, is much more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
verses.kjv: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.
verses.bsb: and to love Him with all your heart and with all your understanding and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself, which is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”

verses.esv: And when Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” And after that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
verses.kjv: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question.
verses.bsb: When Jesus saw that the man had answered wisely, He said, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.”  And no one dared to question Him any further.

verses.esv: And as Jesus taught in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?
verses.bsb: While Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, He asked, “How can the scribes say that the Christ is the Son of David?

verses.esv: David himself, in the Holy Spirit, declared, “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
verses.kjv: For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
verses.bsb: Speaking by the Holy Spirit, David himself declared:  ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand until I put Your enemies under Your feet.”’

verses.esv: David himself calls him Lord. So how is he his son?” And the great throng heard him gladly.
verses.kjv: David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.
verses.bsb: David himself calls Him ‘Lord.’ So how can He be David’s son?”  And the large crowd listened to Him with delight.

verses.esv: And in his teaching he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes and like greetings in the marketplaces
verses.kjv: ¶ And he said unto them in his doctrine, Beware of the scribes, which love to go in long clothing, and love salutations in the marketplaces,
verses.bsb: In His teaching Jesus also said, “Watch out for the scribes. They like to walk around in long robes, to receive greetings in the marketplaces,

verses.esv: and have the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
verses.kjv: And the chief seats in the synagogues, and the uppermost rooms at feasts:
verses.bsb: and to have the chief seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.

verses.esv: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
verses.kjv: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: these shall receive greater damnation.
verses.bsb: They defraud widows of their houses, and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will receive greater condemnation.”

verses.esv: And he sat down opposite the treasury and watched the people putting money into the offering box. Many rich people put in large sums.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus sat over against the treasury, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury: and many that were rich cast in much.
verses.bsb: As Jesus was sitting opposite the treasury, He watched the crowd putting money into it. And many rich people put in large amounts.

verses.esv: And a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which make a penny.
verses.kjv: And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.
verses.bsb: Then one poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which amounted to a small fraction of a denarius.

verses.esv: And he called his disciples to him and said to them, “Truly, I say to you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the offering box.
verses.kjv: And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That this poor widow hath cast more in, than all they which have cast into the treasury:
verses.bsb: Jesus called His disciples to Him and said, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put more than all the others into the treasury.

verses.esv: For they all contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on.”
verses.kjv: For all they did cast in of their abundance; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.
verses.bsb: For they all contributed out of their surplus, but she out of her poverty has put in all she had to live on.”

verses.esv: And as he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!”
verses.kjv: AND as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildings are here!
verses.bsb: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of His disciples said to Him, “Teacher, look at the magnificent stones and buildings!”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
verses.bsb: “Do you see all these great buildings?” Jesus replied. “Not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.”

verses.esv: And as he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,
verses.kjv: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,
verses.bsb: While Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked Him privately,

verses.esv: “Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be accomplished?”
verses.kjv: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?
verses.bsb: “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are about to be fulfilled?”

verses.esv: And Jesus began to say to them, “See that no one leads you astray.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any man deceive you:
verses.bsb: Jesus began by telling them, “See to it that no one deceives you.

verses.esv: Many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and they will lead many astray.
verses.kjv: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.
verses.bsb: Many will come in My name, claiming, ‘I am He,’ and will deceive many.

verses.esv: And when you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. This must take place, but the end is not yet.
verses.kjv: And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for such things must needs be; but the end shall not be yet.
verses.bsb: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.

verses.esv: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. These are but the beginning of the birth pains.
verses.kjv: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrows.
verses.bsb: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, as well as famines. These are the beginning of birth pains.

verses.esv: “But be on your guard. For they will deliver you over to councils, and you will be beaten in synagogues, and you will stand before governors and kings for my sake, to bear witness before them.
verses.kjv: ¶ But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.
verses.bsb: So be on your guard. You will be delivered over to the councils and beaten in the synagogues. On My account you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them.

verses.esv: And the gospel must first be proclaimed to all nations.
verses.kjv: And the gospel must first be published among all nations.
verses.bsb: And the gospel must first be proclaimed to all the nations.

verses.esv: And when they bring you to trial and deliver you over, do not be anxious beforehand what you are to say, but say whatever is given you in that hour, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.
verses.bsb: But when they arrest you and hand you over, do not worry beforehand what to say. Instead, speak whatever you are given at that time, for it will not be you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.

verses.esv: And brother will deliver brother over to death, and the father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death.
verses.kjv: Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.
verses.bsb: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise against their parents and have them put to death.

verses.esv: And you will be hated by all for my name's sake. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.
verses.bsb: You will be hated by everyone because of My name, but the one who perseveres to the end will be saved.

verses.esv: “But when you see the abomination of desolation standing where he ought not to be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
verses.kjv: ¶ But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judæa flee to the mountains:
verses.bsb: So when you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.

verses.esv: Let the one who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter his house, to take anything out,
verses.kjv: And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:
verses.bsb: Let no one on the housetop go back inside to retrieve anything from his house.

verses.esv: and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak.
verses.kjv: And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.
verses.bsb: And let no one in the field return for his cloak.

verses.esv: And alas for women who are pregnant and for those who are nursing infants in those days!
verses.kjv: But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
verses.bsb: How miserable those days will be for pregnant and nursing mothers!

verses.esv: Pray that it may not happen in winter.
verses.kjv: And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.
verses.bsb: Pray that this will not occur in the winter.

verses.esv: For in those days there will be such tribulation as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, and never will be.
verses.kjv: For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.
verses.bsb: For those will be days of tribulation unmatched from the beginning of God’s creation until now, and never to be seen again.

verses.esv: And if the Lord had not cut short the days, no human being would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he shortened the days.
verses.kjv: And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect’s sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.
verses.bsb: If the Lord had not cut short those days, nobody would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, whom He has chosen, He has cut them short.

verses.esv: And then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘Look, there he is!’ do not believe it.
verses.kjv: And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:
verses.bsb: At that time if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘There He is!’ do not believe it.

verses.esv: For false christs and false prophets will arise and perform signs and wonders, to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
verses.kjv: For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.
verses.bsb: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and wonders that would deceive even the elect, if that were possible.

verses.esv: But be on guard; I have told you all things beforehand.
verses.kjv: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.
verses.bsb: So be on your guard; I have told you everything in advance.

verses.esv: “But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
verses.kjv: ¶ But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,
verses.bsb: But in those days, after that tribulation:  ‘The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light;

verses.esv: and the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
verses.kjv: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.
verses.bsb: the stars will fall from the sky, and the powers of the heavens will be shaken.’

verses.esv: And then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
verses.kjv: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
verses.bsb: At that time they will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.

verses.esv: And then he will send out the angels and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
verses.kjv: And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.
verses.bsb: And He will send out the angels to gather His elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.

verses.esv: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
verses.kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:
verses.bsb: Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its branches become tender and sprout leaves, you know that summer is near.

verses.esv: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
verses.kjv: So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.
verses.bsb: So also, when you see these things happening, know that He is near, right at the door.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have happened.

verses.esv: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
verses.kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.
verses.bsb: Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will never pass away.

verses.esv: “But concerning that day or that hour, no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
verses.kjv: ¶ But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.
verses.bsb: No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.

verses.esv: Be on guard, keep awake. For you do not know when the time will come.
verses.kjv: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.
verses.bsb: Be on your guard and stay alert! For you do not know when the appointed time will come.

verses.esv: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his servants in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to stay awake.
verses.kjv: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.
verses.bsb: It is like a man going on a journey who left his house, put each servant in charge of his own task, and instructed the doorkeeper to keep watch.

verses.esv: Therefore stay awake—for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning—
verses.kjv: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:
verses.bsb: Therefore keep watch, because you do not know when the master of the house will return—whether in the evening, at midnight, when the rooster crows, or in the morning.

verses.esv: lest he come suddenly and find you asleep.
verses.kjv: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.
verses.bsb: Otherwise, he may arrive without notice and find you sleeping.

verses.esv: And what I say to you I say to all: Stay awake.”
verses.kjv: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
verses.bsb: And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Keep watch!”

verses.esv: It was now two days before the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread. And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how to arrest him by stealth and kill him,
verses.kjv: AFTER two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.
verses.bsb: Now the Passover and the Feast of Unleavened Bread were two days away, and the chief priests and scribes were looking for a covert way to arrest Jesus and kill Him.

verses.esv: for they said, “Not during the feast, lest there be an uproar from the people.”
verses.kjv: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.
verses.bsb: “But not during the feast,” they said, “or there may be a riot among the people.”

verses.esv: And while he was at Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he was reclining at table, a woman came with an alabaster flask of ointment of pure nard, very costly, and she broke the flask and poured it over his head.
verses.kjv: ¶ And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was in Bethany reclining at the table in the home of Simon the Leper, a woman came with an alabaster jar of expensive perfume, made of pure nard. She broke open the jar and poured it on Jesus’ head.

verses.esv: There were some who said to themselves indignantly, “Why was the ointment wasted like that?
verses.kjv: And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment made?
verses.bsb: Some of those present, however, expressed their indignation to one another: “Why this waste of perfume?

verses.esv: For this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor.” And they scolded her.
verses.kjv: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.
verses.bsb: It could have been sold for over three hundred denarii and the money given to the poor.” And they scolded her.

verses.esv: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone. Why do you trouble her? She has done a beautiful thing to me.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.
verses.bsb: But Jesus said, “Leave her alone; why are you bothering her? She has done a beautiful deed to Me.

verses.esv: For you always have the poor with you, and whenever you want, you can do good for them. But you will not always have me.
verses.kjv: For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.
verses.bsb: The poor you will always have with you, and you can help them whenever you want. But you will not always have Me.

verses.esv: She has done what she could; she has anointed my body beforehand for burial.
verses.kjv: She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.
verses.bsb: She has done what she could to anoint My body in advance of My burial.

verses.esv: And truly, I say to you, wherever the gospel is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will be told in memory of her.”
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.
verses.bsb: And truly I tell you, wherever the gospel is preached in all the world, what she has done will also be told in memory of her.”

verses.esv: Then Judas Iscariot, who was one of the twelve, went to the chief priests in order to betray him to them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.
verses.bsb: Then Judas Iscariot, one of the Twelve, went to the chief priests to betray Jesus to them.

verses.esv: And when they heard it, they were glad and promised to give him money. And he sought an opportunity to betray him.
verses.kjv: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him.
verses.bsb: They were delighted to hear this, and they promised to give him money.  So Judas began to look for an opportunity to betray Jesus.

verses.esv: And on the first day of Unleavened Bread, when they sacrificed the Passover lamb, his disciples said to him, “Where will you have us go and prepare for you to eat the Passover?”
verses.kjv: ¶ And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover, his disciples said unto him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?
verses.bsb: On the first day of the Feast of Unleavened Bread, when the Passover lamb was to be sacrificed, Jesus’ disciples asked Him, “Where do You want us to prepare for You to eat the Passover?”

verses.esv: And he sent two of his disciples and said to them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him,
verses.kjv: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.
verses.bsb: So He sent two of His disciples and told them, “Go into the city, and a man carrying a jug of water will meet you. Follow him,

verses.esv: and wherever he enters, say to the master of the house, ‘The Teacher says, Where is my guest room, where I may eat the Passover with my disciples?’
verses.kjv: And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
verses.bsb: and whichever house he enters, say to the owner, ‘The Teacher asks: Where is My guest room, where I may eat the Passover with My disciples?’

verses.esv: And he will show you a large upper room furnished and ready; there prepare for us.”
verses.kjv: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.
verses.bsb: And he will show you a large upper room, furnished and ready. Make preparations for us there.”

verses.esv: And the disciples set out and went to the city and found it just as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
verses.kjv: And his disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
verses.bsb: So the disciples left and went into the city, where they found everything as Jesus had described. And they prepared the Passover.

verses.esv: And when it was evening, he came with the twelve.
verses.kjv: And in the evening he cometh with the twelve.
verses.bsb: When evening came, Jesus arrived with the Twelve.

verses.esv: And as they were reclining at table and eating, Jesus said, “Truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me, one who is eating with me.”
verses.kjv: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.
verses.bsb: And while they were reclining and eating, Jesus said, “Truly I tell you, one of you who is eating with Me will betray Me.”

verses.esv: They began to be sorrowful and to say to him one after another, “Is it I?”
verses.kjv: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, Is it I? and another said, Is it I?
verses.bsb: They began to be grieved and to ask Him one after another, “Surely not I?”

verses.esv: He said to them, “It is one of the twelve, one who is dipping bread into the dish with me.
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.
verses.bsb: He answered, “It is one of the Twelve—the one who is dipping his hand into the bowl with Me.

verses.esv: For the Son of Man goes as it is written of him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been better for that man if he had not been born.”
verses.kjv: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.
verses.bsb: The Son of Man will go just as it is written about Him, but woe to that man by whom He is betrayed! It would be better for him if he had not been born.”

verses.esv: And as they were eating, he took bread, and after blessing it broke it and gave it to them, and said, “Take; this is my body.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.
verses.bsb: While they were eating, Jesus took bread, spoke a blessing and broke it, and gave it to the disciples, saying, “Take it; this is My body.”

verses.esv: And he took a cup, and when he had given thanks he gave it to them, and they all drank of it.
verses.kjv: And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them: and they all drank of it.
verses.bsb: Then He took the cup, gave thanks, and gave it to them, and they all drank from it.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.
verses.bsb: He said to them, “This is My blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.

verses.esv: Truly, I say to you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.”
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.
verses.bsb: Truly I tell you, I will no longer drink of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it anew in the kingdom of God.”

verses.esv: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.
verses.bsb: And when they had sung a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to them, “You will all fall away, for it is written, ‘I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.
verses.bsb: Then Jesus said to them, “You will all fall away, for it is written:  ‘I will strike the Shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’

verses.esv: But after I am raised up, I will go before you to Galilee.”
verses.kjv: But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.
verses.bsb: But after I have risen, I will go ahead of you into Galilee.”

verses.esv: Peter said to him, “Even though they all fall away, I will not.”
verses.kjv: But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.
verses.bsb: Peter declared, “Even if all fall away, I never will.”

verses.esv: And Jesus said to him, “Truly, I tell you, this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.
verses.bsb: “Truly I tell you,” Jesus replied, “this very night, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times.”

verses.esv: But he said emphatically, “If I must die with you, I will not deny you.” And they all said the same.
verses.kjv: But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not deny thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.
verses.bsb: But Peter kept insisting, “Even if I have to die with You, I will never deny You.” And all the others said the same thing.

verses.esv: And they went to a place called Gethsemane. And he said to his disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”
verses.kjv: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, Sit ye here, while I shall pray.
verses.bsb: Then they came to a place called Gethsemane, and Jesus told His disciples, “Sit here while I pray.”

verses.esv: And he took with him Peter and James and John, and began to be greatly distressed and troubled.
verses.kjv: And he taketh with him Peter and James and John, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heavy;
verses.bsb: He took with Him Peter, James, and John, and began to be deeply troubled and distressed.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “My soul is very sorrowful, even to death. Remain here and watch.”
verses.kjv: And saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.
verses.bsb: Then He said to them, “My soul is consumed with sorrow to the point of death. Stay here and keep watch.”

verses.esv: And going a little farther, he fell on the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
verses.kjv: And he went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.
verses.bsb: Going a little farther, He fell to the ground and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour would pass from Him.

verses.esv: And he said, “Abba, Father, all things are possible for you. Remove this cup from me. Yet not what I will, but what you will.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Abba, Father, all things are possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.
verses.bsb: “Abba, Father,” He said, “all things are possible for You. Take this cup from Me. Yet not what I will, but what You will.”

verses.esv: And he came and found them sleeping, and he said to Peter, “Simon, are you asleep? Could you not watch one hour?
verses.kjv: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?
verses.bsb: Then Jesus returned and found them sleeping. “Simon, are you asleep?” He asked. “Were you not able to keep watch for one hour?

verses.esv: Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation. The spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.”
verses.kjv: Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.
verses.bsb: Watch and pray so that you will not enter into temptation. For the spirit is willing, but the body is weak.”

verses.esv: And again he went away and prayed, saying the same words.
verses.kjv: And again he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.
verses.bsb: Again He went away and prayed, saying the same thing.

verses.esv: And again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were very heavy, and they did not know what to answer him.
verses.kjv: And when he returned, he found them asleep again, (for their eyes were heavy,) neither wist they what to answer him.
verses.bsb: And again Jesus returned and found them sleeping—for their eyes were heavy. And they did not know what to answer Him.

verses.esv: And he came the third time and said to them, “Are you still sleeping and taking your rest? It is enough; the hour has come. The Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
verses.kjv: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.
verses.bsb: When Jesus returned the third time, He said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? That is enough! The hour has come. Look, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

verses.esv: Rise, let us be going; see, my betrayer is at hand.”
verses.kjv: Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.
verses.bsb: Rise, let us go. See, My betrayer is approaching!”

verses.esv: And immediately, while he was still speaking, Judas came, one of the twelve, and with him a crowd with swords and clubs, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
verses.kjv: ¶ And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.
verses.bsb: While Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the Twelve, arrived, accompanied by a crowd armed with swords and clubs, sent from the chief priests, scribes, and elders.

verses.esv: Now the betrayer had given them a sign, saying, “The one I will kiss is the man. Seize him and lead him away under guard.”
verses.kjv: And he that betrayed him had given them a token, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he; take him, and lead him away safely.
verses.bsb: Now the betrayer had arranged a signal with them: “The One I kiss is the man; arrest Him and lead Him away securely.”

verses.esv: And when he came, he went up to him at once and said, “Rabbi!” And he kissed him.
verses.kjv: And as soon as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and saith, Master, master; and kissed him.
verses.bsb: Going directly to Jesus, he said, “Rabbi!” and kissed Him.

verses.esv: And they laid hands on him and seized him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they laid their hands on him, and took him.
verses.bsb: Then the men seized Jesus and arrested Him.

verses.esv: But one of those who stood by drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
verses.kjv: And one of them that stood by drew a sword, and smote a servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
verses.bsb: And one of the bystanders drew his sword and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his ear.

verses.esv: And Jesus said to them, “Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to capture me?
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?
verses.bsb: Jesus asked the crowd, “Have you come out with swords and clubs to arrest Me as you would an outlaw?

verses.esv: Day after day I was with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me. But let the Scriptures be fulfilled.”
verses.kjv: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.
verses.bsb: Every day I was with you, teaching in the temple courts, and you did not arrest Me. But this has happened that the Scriptures would be fulfilled.”

verses.esv: And they all left him and fled.
verses.kjv: And they all forsook him, and fled.
verses.bsb: Then everyone deserted Him and fled.

verses.esv: And a young man followed him, with nothing but a linen cloth about his body. And they seized him,
verses.kjv: And there followed him a certain young man, having a linen cloth cast about his naked body; and the young men laid hold on him:
verses.bsb: One young man who had been following Jesus was wearing a linen cloth around his body. They caught hold of him,

verses.esv: but he left the linen cloth and ran away naked.
verses.kjv: And he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.
verses.bsb: but he pulled free of the linen cloth and ran away naked.

verses.esv: And they led Jesus to the high priest. And all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes came together.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.
verses.bsb: They led Jesus away to the high priest, and all the chief priests, elders, and scribes assembled.

verses.esv: And Peter had followed him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. And he was sitting with the guards and warming himself at the fire.
verses.kjv: And Peter followed him afar off, even into the palace of the high priest: and he sat with the servants, and warmed himself at the fire.
verses.bsb: Peter followed Him at a distance, right into the courtyard of the high priest. And he sat with the officers and warmed himself by the fire.

verses.esv: Now the chief priests and the whole council were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they found none.
verses.kjv: And the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against Jesus to put him to death; and found none.
verses.bsb: Now the chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were seeking testimony against Jesus to put Him to death, but they did not find any.

verses.esv: For many bore false witness against him, but their testimony did not agree.
verses.kjv: For many bare false witness against him, but their witness agreed not together.
verses.bsb: For many bore false witness against Jesus, but their testimony was inconsistent.

verses.esv: And some stood up and bore false witness against him, saying,
verses.kjv: And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,
verses.bsb: Then some men stood up and testified falsely against Him:

verses.esv: “We heard him say, ‘I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and in three days I will build another, not made with hands.’”
verses.kjv: We heard him say, I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.
verses.bsb: “We heard Him say, ‘I will destroy this man-made temple, and in three days I will build another that is made without hands.’”

verses.esv: Yet even about this their testimony did not agree.
verses.kjv: But neither so did their witness agree together.
verses.bsb: But even their testimony was inconsistent.

verses.esv: And the high priest stood up in the midst and asked Jesus, “Have you no answer to make? What is it that these men testify against you?”
verses.kjv: And the high priest stood up in the midst, and asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?
verses.bsb: So the high priest stood up before them and questioned Jesus, “Have You no answer? What are these men testifying against You?”

verses.esv: But he remained silent and made no answer. Again the high priest asked him, “Are you the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?”
verses.kjv: But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and said unto him, Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?
verses.bsb: But Jesus remained silent and made no reply.  Again the high priest questioned Him, “Are You the Christ, the Son of the Blessed One?”

verses.esv: And Jesus said, “I am, and you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power, and coming with the clouds of heaven.”
verses.kjv: And Jesus said, I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.
verses.bsb: “I am,” said Jesus, “and you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of Power and coming with the clouds of heaven.”

verses.esv: And the high priest tore his garments and said, “What further witnesses do we need?
verses.kjv: Then the high priest rent his clothes, and saith, What need we any further witnesses?
verses.bsb: At this, the high priest tore his clothes and declared, “Why do we need any more witnesses?

verses.esv: You have heard his blasphemy. What is your decision?” And they all condemned him as deserving death.
verses.kjv: Ye have heard the blasphemy: what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.
verses.bsb: You have heard the blasphemy. What is your verdict?”  And they all condemned Him as deserving of death.

verses.esv: And some began to spit on him and to cover his face and to strike him, saying to him, “Prophesy!” And the guards received him with blows.
verses.kjv: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.
verses.bsb: Then some of them began to spit on Him. They blindfolded Him, struck Him with their fists, and said to Him, “Prophesy!” And the officers received Him with slaps in His face.

verses.esv: And as Peter was below in the courtyard, one of the servant girls of the high priest came,
verses.kjv: ¶ And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:
verses.bsb: While Peter was in the courtyard below, one of the servant girls of the high priest came down

verses.esv: and seeing Peter warming himself, she looked at him and said, “You also were with the Nazarene, Jesus.”
verses.kjv: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.
verses.bsb: and saw him warming himself there. She looked at Peter and said, “You also were with Jesus the Nazarene.”

verses.esv: But he denied it, saying, “I neither know nor understand what you mean.” And he went out into the gateway and the rooster crowed.
verses.kjv: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.
verses.bsb: But he denied it. “I do not know or even understand what you are talking about,” he said. Then he went out to the gateway, and the rooster crowed.

verses.esv: And the servant girl saw him and began again to say to the bystanders, “This man is one of them.”
verses.kjv: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.
verses.bsb: There the servant girl saw him and again said to those standing nearby, “This man is one of them.”

verses.esv: But again he denied it. And after a little while the bystanders again said to Peter, “Certainly you are one of them, for you are a Galilean.”
verses.kjv: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilæan, and thy speech agreeth thereto.
verses.bsb: But he denied it again.  After a little while, those standing nearby said once more to Peter, “Surely you are one of them, for you too are a Galilean.”

verses.esv: But he began to invoke a curse on himself and to swear, “I do not know this man of whom you speak.”
verses.kjv: But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know not this man of whom ye speak.
verses.bsb: But he began to curse and swear, “I do not know this man of whom you speak!”

verses.esv: And immediately the rooster crowed a second time. And Peter remembered how Jesus had said to him, “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times.” And he broke down and wept.
verses.kjv: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.
verses.bsb: And immediately the rooster crowed a second time.  Then Peter remembered the word that Jesus had spoken to him: “Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny Me three times.” And he broke down and wept.

verses.esv: And as soon as it was morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council. And they bound Jesus and led him away and delivered him over to Pilate.
verses.kjv: AND straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.
verses.bsb: Early in the morning, the chief priests, elders, scribes, and the whole Sanhedrin devised a plan. They bound Jesus, led Him away, and handed Him over to Pilate.

verses.esv: And Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” And he answered him, “You have said so.”
verses.kjv: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.
verses.bsb: So Pilate questioned Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”  “You have said so,” Jesus replied.

verses.esv: And the chief priests accused him of many things.
verses.kjv: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.
verses.bsb: And the chief priests began to accuse Him of many things.

verses.esv: And Pilate again asked him, “Have you no answer to make? See how many charges they bring against you.”
verses.kjv: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.
verses.bsb: Then Pilate questioned Him again, “Have You no answer? Look how many charges they are bringing against You!”

verses.esv: But Jesus made no further answer, so that Pilate was amazed.
verses.kjv: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.
verses.bsb: But to Pilate’s amazement, Jesus made no further reply.

verses.esv: Now at the feast he used to release for them one prisoner for whom they asked.
verses.kjv: Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.
verses.bsb: Now it was Pilate’s custom at the feast to release to the people a prisoner of their choosing.

verses.esv: And among the rebels in prison, who had committed murder in the insurrection, there was a man called Barabbas.
verses.kjv: And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.
verses.bsb: And a man named Barabbas was imprisoned with the rebels who had committed murder during the insurrection.

verses.esv: And the crowd came up and began to ask Pilate to do as he usually did for them.
verses.kjv: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.
verses.bsb: So the crowd went up and began asking Pilate to keep his custom.

verses.esv: And he answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release for you the King of the Jews?”
verses.kjv: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
verses.bsb: “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?” Pilate asked.

verses.esv: For he perceived that it was out of envy that the chief priests had delivered him up.
verses.kjv: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.
verses.bsb: For he knew it was out of envy that the chief priests had handed Jesus over.

verses.esv: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release for them Barabbas instead.
verses.kjv: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.
verses.bsb: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd to have him release Barabbas to them instead.

verses.esv: And Pilate again said to them, “Then what shall I do with the man you call the King of the Jews?”
verses.kjv: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?
verses.bsb: So Pilate asked them again, “What then do you want me to do with the One you call the King of the Jews?”

verses.esv: And they cried out again, “Crucify him.”
verses.kjv: And they cried out again, Crucify him.
verses.bsb: And they shouted back, “Crucify Him!”

verses.esv: And Pilate said to them, “Why? What evil has he done?” But they shouted all the more, “Crucify him.”
verses.kjv: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.
verses.bsb: “Why?” asked Pilate. “What evil has He done?”  But they shouted all the louder, “Crucify Him!”

verses.esv: So Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released for them Barabbas, and having scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.
verses.kjv: ¶ And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.
verses.bsb: And wishing to satisfy the crowd, Pilate released Barabbas to them. But he had Jesus flogged, and handed Him over to be crucified.

verses.esv: And the soldiers led him away inside the palace (that is, the governor's headquarters), and they called together the whole battalion.
verses.kjv: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Prætorium; and they call together the whole band.
verses.bsb: Then the soldiers led Jesus away into the palace (that is, the Praetorium) and called the whole company together.

verses.esv: And they clothed him in a purple cloak, and twisting together a crown of thorns, they put it on him.
verses.kjv: And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,
verses.bsb: They dressed Him in a purple robe, twisted together a crown of thorns, and set it on His head.

verses.esv: And they began to salute him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
verses.kjv: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!
verses.bsb: And they began to salute Him: “Hail, King of the Jews!”

verses.esv: And they were striking his head with a reed and spitting on him and kneeling down in homage to him.
verses.kjv: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.
verses.bsb: They kept striking His head with a staff and spitting on Him. And they knelt down and bowed before Him.

verses.esv: And when they had mocked him, they stripped him of the purple cloak and put his own clothes on him. And they led him out to crucify him.
verses.kjv: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.
verses.bsb: After they had mocked Him, they removed the purple robe and put His own clothes back on Him. Then they led Him out to crucify Him.

verses.esv: And they compelled a passerby, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in from the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to carry his cross.
verses.kjv: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.
verses.bsb: Now Simon of Cyrene, the father of Alexander and Rufus, was passing by on his way in from the country, and the soldiers forced him to carry the cross of Jesus.

verses.esv: And they brought him to the place called Golgotha (which means Place of a Skull).
verses.kjv: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.
verses.bsb: They brought Jesus to a place called Golgotha, which means The Place of the Skull.

verses.esv: And they offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.
verses.kjv: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.
verses.bsb: There they offered Him wine mixed with myrrh, but He did not take it.

verses.esv: And they crucified him and divided his garments among them, casting lots for them, to decide what each should take.
verses.kjv: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.
verses.bsb: And they crucified Him.  They also divided His garments by casting lots to decide what each of them would take.

verses.esv: And it was the third hour when they crucified him.
verses.kjv: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.
verses.bsb: It was the third hour when they crucified Him.

verses.esv: And the inscription of the charge against him read, “The King of the Jews.”
verses.kjv: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.
verses.bsb: And the charge inscribed against Him read:  THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.esv: And with him they crucified two robbers, one on his right and one on his left.
verses.kjv: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.
verses.bsb: Along with Jesus, they crucified two robbers, one on His right and one on His left.

verses.esv: And those who passed by derided him, wagging their heads and saying, “Aha! You who would destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,
verses.kjv: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,
verses.bsb: And those who passed by heaped abuse on Him, shaking their heads and saying, “Aha! You who are going to destroy the temple and rebuild it in three days,

verses.esv: save yourself, and come down from the cross!”
verses.kjv: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.
verses.bsb: come down from the cross and save Yourself!”

verses.esv: So also the chief priests with the scribes mocked him to one another, saying, “He saved others; he cannot save himself.
verses.kjv: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.
verses.bsb: In the same way, the chief priests and scribes mocked Him among themselves, saying, “He saved others, but He cannot save Himself!

verses.esv: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross that we may see and believe.” Those who were crucified with him also reviled him.
verses.kjv: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.
verses.bsb: Let the Christ, the King of Israel, come down now from the cross, so that we may see and believe!” And even those who were crucified with Him berated Him.

verses.esv: And when the sixth hour had come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
verses.kjv: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.
verses.bsb: From the sixth hour until the ninth hour darkness came over all the land.

verses.esv: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?”
verses.kjv: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?
verses.bsb: At the ninth hour, Jesus cried out in a loud voice, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?”

verses.esv: And some of the bystanders hearing it said, “Behold, he is calling Elijah.”
verses.kjv: And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.
verses.bsb: When some of those standing nearby heard this, they said, “Behold, He is calling Elijah.”

verses.esv: And someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine, put it on a reed and gave it to him to drink, saying, “Wait, let us see whether Elijah will come to take him down.”
verses.kjv: And one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.
verses.bsb: And someone ran and filled a sponge with sour wine. He put it on a reed and held it up for Jesus to drink, saying, “Leave Him alone. Let us see if Elijah comes to take Him down.”

verses.esv: And Jesus uttered a loud cry and breathed his last.
verses.kjv: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.
verses.bsb: But Jesus let out a loud cry and breathed His last.

verses.esv: And the curtain of the temple was torn in two, from top to bottom.
verses.kjv: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.
verses.bsb: And the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.

verses.esv: And when the centurion, who stood facing him, saw that in this way he breathed his last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”
verses.kjv: ¶ And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.
