verses.bsb: When the centurion standing there in front of Jesus saw how He had breathed His last, he said, “Truly this man was the Son of God!”

verses.esv: There were also women looking on from a distance, among whom were Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
verses.kjv: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;
verses.bsb: And there were also women watching from a distance. Among them were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.

verses.esv: When he was in Galilee, they followed him and ministered to him, and there were also many other women who came up with him to Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: These women had followed Jesus and ministered to Him while He was in Galilee, and there were many other women who had come up to Jerusalem with Him.

verses.esv: And when evening had come, since it was the day of Preparation, that is, the day before the Sabbath,
verses.kjv: ¶ And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,
verses.bsb: Now it was already evening. Since it was Preparation Day (that is, the day before the Sabbath),

verses.esv: Joseph of Arimathea, a respected member of the council, who was also himself looking for the kingdom of God, took courage and went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
verses.kjv: Joseph of Arimathæa, an honourable counseller, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.
verses.bsb: Joseph of Arimathea, a prominent Council member who himself was waiting for the kingdom of God, boldly went to Pilate to ask for the body of Jesus.

verses.esv: Pilate was surprised to hear that he should have already died. And summoning the centurion, he asked him whether he was already dead.
verses.kjv: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling unto him the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.
verses.bsb: Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, so he summoned the centurion to ask if this was so.

verses.esv: And when he learned from the centurion that he was dead, he granted the corpse to Joseph.
verses.kjv: And when he knew it of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.
verses.bsb: When Pilate had confirmed it with the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.

verses.esv: And Joseph bought a linen shroud, and taking him down, wrapped him in the linen shroud and laid him in a tomb that had been cut out of the rock. And he rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.
verses.kjv: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.
verses.bsb: So Joseph bought a linen cloth, took down the body of Jesus, wrapped it in the cloth, and placed it in a tomb that had been cut out of the rock. Then he rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.

verses.esv: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses saw where he was laid.
verses.kjv: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses beheld where he was laid.
verses.bsb: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph saw where His body was placed.

verses.esv: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.
verses.kjv: AND when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.
verses.bsb: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so they could go and anoint the body of Jesus.

verses.esv: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.
verses.kjv: And very early in the morning the first day of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.
verses.bsb: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they went to the tomb.

verses.esv: And they were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance of the tomb?”
verses.kjv: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?
verses.bsb: They were asking one another, “Who will roll away the stone from the entrance of the tomb?”

verses.esv: And looking up, they saw that the stone had been rolled back—it was very large.
verses.kjv: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.
verses.bsb: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, even though it was extremely large.

verses.esv: And entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were alarmed.
verses.kjv: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.
verses.bsb: When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were alarmed.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has risen; he is not here. See the place where they laid him.
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.
verses.bsb: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here! See the place where they put Him.

verses.esv: But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he is going before you to Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.”
verses.kjv: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
verses.bsb: But go, tell His disciples and Peter, ‘He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see Him, just as He told you.’”

verses.esv: And they went out and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had seized them, and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid. [Some of the earliest manuscripts do not include 16:9–20.] [[Now when he rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
verses.kjv: And they went out quickly, and fled from the sepulchre; for they trembled and were amazed: neither said they any thing to any man; for they were afraid.
verses.bsb: So the women left the tomb and ran away, trembling and bewildered. And in their fear they did not say a word to anyone.

verses.esv: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
verses.kjv: And she went and told them that had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
verses.bsb: She went and told those who had been with Him, who were mourning and weeping.

verses.esv: But when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they would not believe it.
verses.kjv: And they, when they had heard that he was alive, and had been seen of her, believed not.
verses.bsb: And when they heard that Jesus was alive and she had seen Him, they did not believe it.

verses.esv: After these things he appeared in another form to two of them, as they were walking into the country.
verses.kjv: ¶ After that he appeared in another form unto two of them, as they walked, and went into the country.
verses.bsb: After this, Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them as they walked along in the country.

verses.esv: And they went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.
verses.kjv: And they went and told it unto the residue: neither believed they them.
verses.bsb: And they went back and reported it to the rest, but they did not believe them either.

verses.esv: Afterward he appeared to the eleven themselves as they were reclining at table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.
verses.kjv: ¶ Afterward he appeared unto the eleven as they sat at meat, and upbraided them with their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they believed not them which had seen him after he was risen.
verses.bsb: Later, as they were eating, Jesus appeared to the Eleven and rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who had seen Him after He had risen.

verses.esv: And he said to them, “Go into all the world and proclaim the gospel to the whole creation.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Go ye into all the world, and preach the gospel to every creature.
verses.bsb: And He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.

verses.esv: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
verses.kjv: He that believeth and is baptized shall be saved; but he that believeth not shall be damned.
verses.bsb: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.

verses.esv: And these signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
verses.kjv: And these signs shall follow them that believe; In my name shall they cast out devils; they shall speak with new tongues;
verses.bsb: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In My name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues;

verses.esv: they will pick up serpents with their hands; and if they drink any deadly poison, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
verses.kjv: They shall take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it shall not hurt them; they shall lay hands on the sick, and they shall recover.
verses.bsb: they will pick up snakes with their hands, and if they drink any deadly poison, it will not harm them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will be made well.”

verses.esv: So then the Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
verses.kjv: ¶ So then after the Lord had spoken unto them, he was received up into heaven, and sat on the right hand of God.
verses.bsb: After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, He was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.

verses.esv: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by accompanying signs.]]
verses.kjv: And they went forth, and preached every where, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word with signs following. Amen.
verses.bsb: And they went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked through them, confirming His word by the signs that accompanied it.
